GT LEARN #139 🧮 Word Break — DP MEDIUM DP String HashSet
expected: True

📝 Problem Statement

Given ek string s aur ek list wordDict. Hume check karna hai ki s ko space-separated sequence mein todh kar represent kiya ja sakta hai aise ki har word wordDict mein mile. Ek hi word ko multiple times use kar sakte hain. Return True ya False.

🧠 Solving Approach (Bottom-Up DP)

Define dp[i] = True iff prefix s[0:i] ko valid words mein todha ja sakta hai. Base: dp[0]=True (empty string). Transition: dp[i] = True agar koi j < i ho jahan dp[j]=True AND s[j:i] dictionary mein hai. Answer: dp[n].

🎯 Core Concepts

Statedp[i] = can s[0:i] be segmented?
TransitionFor each split point j, check dp[j] AND s[j:i] ∈ set.
Early BreakJaise hi koi valid split mile, andar wala loop break — efficiency boost.

⏱ Complexity

Time: O(n² · L) Space: O(n + dict)

🧪 Test Cases

T1classic 2-word split→ True
s="leetcode"dict=["leet","code"]
T2word reused (apple twice)→ True
s="applepenapple"dict=["apple","pen"]
T3dead-end trap (no valid)→ False
s="catsandog"dict=["cats","dog","sand","and","cat"]
T4overlapping splits→ True
s="aaaaaaa"dict=["aaa","aaaa"]
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization
0/0
←→R 100%
